parts: Name this medical field founded by Patrick Manson. A London school named for hygiene and this field acknowledges its role in “ensuring white men could survive while running the colonies.”

After discovering malaria transmission via mosquitos, Ronald Ross chaired a tropical medicine institute in this city. By the end of the 1700s, this British city was the largest port in Europe’s slave trade.
German-Dutch physician Franz Junghuhn cultivated quinine-yielding cinchona plantations on this island, where the Dutch East India Company established its capital of Batavia, now modern-day Jakarta.
